assumptions: A declared intragroup dividend recognised as receivable is analysed as a recognised intragroup monetary asset for IFRS 9 hedged-item assessment.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup dividend receivable is most directly tested under the fair value hedge model, but only if the intragroup monetary-item exception for foreign currency risk is met in consolidation.

approaches.label: Ineligible hedged item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 sets the default rule that intragroup items are not eligible hedged items in consolidated financial statements unless a specific exception applies.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: If the exposure is considered before recognition, only a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction whose foreign currency risk affects consolidated profit or loss can qualify under this hedge model.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 provide a separate hedge model for foreign currency risk on net investments in foreign operations, which defines an important boundary from ordinary intragroup receivables.
